metadata.description: Pilot calibration set for LLMMC estimates, covering low/mid/high effectiveness range

anchors.description: Automatic enrollment in 401(k) retirement savings plan
anchors.effect_detail: Participation increased from 49% to 86% (new hires) and from 37% to 86% (existing)
anchors.source_full: Madrian, B. C., & Shea, D. F. (2001). The power of suggestion: Inertia in 401(k) participation and savings behavior. The Quarterly Journal of Economics, 116(4), 1149-1187.
Johnson, E. J., & Goldstein, D. (2003). Do defaults save lives? Science, 302(5649), 1338-1339.

anchors.description: Green energy as default electricity option
anchors.effect_detail: Green energy adoption ~90% with opt-out vs ~45% with opt-in
anchors.source_full: Ebeling, F., & Lotz, S. (2015). Domestic uptake of green energy promoted by opt-out tariffs. Nature Climate Change, 5(9), 868-871.

anchors.description: Save More Tomorrow automatic escalation of savings rate
anchors.effect_detail: Savings rate increased from 3.5% to 13.6% over 4 years (10.1pp increase, but effect = high participation in escalation)
anchors.source_full: Thaler, R. H., & Benartzi, S. (2004). Save more tomorrow: Using behavioral economics to increase employee saving. Journal of Political Economy, 112(S1), S164-S187.

anchors.effect_detail: ~2 additional gym visits per week during incentive period
anchors.source_full: Charness, G., & Gneezy, U. (2009). Incentives to exercise. Econometrica, 77(3), 909-931.

anchors.description: Deposit contracts for weight loss (lose money if fail)
anchors.effect_detail: ~3kg additional weight loss with deposit contract
anchors.source_full: Royer, H., Stehr, M., & Sydnor, J. (2015). Incentives, commitments, and habit formation in exercise. American Economic Journal: Applied Economics, 7(3), 66-99.

anchors.effect_detail: 4-6pp increase in vaccination rates from reminders
anchors.source_full: Milkman, K. L., et al. (2021). A megastudy of text-based nudges encouraging patients to get vaccinated at an upcoming doctor's appointment. PNAS, 118(20).

anchors.description: Implementation intention prompts for flu vaccination
anchors.effect_detail: Writing down date/time increased vaccination by 4.2pp
anchors.source_full: Milkman, K. L., Beshears, J., Choi, J. J., Laibson, D., & Madrian, B. C. (2011). Using implementation intentions prompts to enhance influenza vaccination rates. PNAS, 108(26), 10415-10420.

anchors.description: Real-time electricity usage feedback via in-home display
anchors.effect_detail: 5-15% reduction in electricity consumption with real-time feedback
anchors.source_full: Jessoe, K., & Rapson, D. (2014). Knowledge is (less) power: Experimental evidence from residential energy use. American Economic Review, 104(4), 1417-1438.

anchors.description: Home energy reports comparing usage to neighbors
anchors.effect_detail: Average 2% reduction in energy use from Opower reports
anchors.source_full: Allcott, H. (2011). Social norms and energy conservation. Journal of Public Economics, 95(9-10), 1082-1095.

anchors.description: Calorie labeling at point of purchase (Starbucks)
anchors.source_full: Bollinger, B., Leslie, P., & Sorensen, A. (2011). Calorie posting in chain restaurants. American Economic Journal: Economic Policy, 3(1), 91-128.

anchors.description: Loss vs. gain framing for energy conservation messages
anchors.effect_detail: Small framing effect on conservation intentions and behavior
anchors.source_full: Gonzales, M. H., Aronson, E., & Costanzo, M. A. (1988). Using social cognition and persuasion to promote energy conservation. Journal of Applied Social Psychology, 18(12), 1049-1066.

anchors.description: Information about retirement savings (no behavioral intervention)
anchors.effect_detail: Information-only interventions show minimal effect on savings behavior
anchors.source_full: Choi, J. J., Laibson, D., Madrian, B. C., & Metrick, A. (2012). Defined contribution pensions: Plan rules, participant choices, and the path of least resistance. Tax Policy and the Economy, 16, 67-113.

anchors.effect_detail: Combined effect exceeds sum of individual effects by ~8pp
anchors.source_full: Benartzi, S., & Thaler, R. H. (2013). Behavioral economics and the retirement savings crisis. Science, 339(6124), 1152-1153.

anchors.description: Descriptive norm + injunctive norm (smiley face) combination
anchors.effect_detail: Injunctive norm prevents boomerang effect in low-users
anchors.source_full: Schultz, P. W., Nolan, J. M., Cialdini, R. B., Goldstein, N. J., & Griskevicius, V. (2007). The constructive, destructive, and reconstructive power of social norms. Psychological Science, 18(5), 429-434.

anchors.description: Negative interaction: monetary incentive crowds out intrinsic motivation
anchors.effect_detail: Introducing fines for late pickup INCREASED lateness (crowding out)
anchors.source_full: Gneezy, U., & Rustichini, A. (2000). A fine is a price. The Journal of Legal Studies, 29(1), 1-17.

llmmc_placeholder._note: LLMMC estimates to be filled in after running HHH-LLMMC-1 protocol

next_steps: 1. Run LLMMC protocol for each anchor to get theta_llm and eu_llm
4. If validation passes, freeze as calibration_v1
